title: Poolside Pavilion Architect
description: Design a futuristic poolside pavilion by positioning architectural columns (robot sentinels wearing sunglasses) around a central pool. Users place, rotate, scale, and reorder robotic column guardians along a parametric curve surrounding the pool, while maintaining a fixed total structural mass budget. Undo/redo support lets users iterate freely on their pavilion layout.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a poolside pavilion architecture tool. Load pool.glb centered at the origin as the main architectural feature. Load robot.glb as a clonable sentinel column asset and SunglassesKhronos.glb attached to each robot's head bone or top mesh as decorative detail. Camera: OrbitControls targeting the pool, initial position elevated at 45 degrees, distance 12 units. Lighting: ambient light 0.4 intensity, directional sun light casting shadows onto a ground plane, plus a subtle blue point light inside the pool. Interactions (10-14 steps expected): 1) Click an Add Column button to spawn a robot+sunglasses pair at a default position on a circular path around the pool. 2) Click any placed robot to select it (highlight with outline or emissive color). 3) Drag selected robot along the circular parametric path (constrained). 4) Use a Scale slider (0.5x-2.0x) to resize the selected robot, which changes its mass proportionally (mass = scale cubed). 5) Enforce a conservation law: total mass budget of 50 units across all placed robots. Display a mass budget bar. If adding or scaling would exceed budget, clamp or reject the action. 6) Rotate selected robot around Y-axis with a rotation slider. 7) Toggle sunglasses visibility per robot with a checkbox. 8) Delete selected robot with a Remove button, freeing its mass. 9) Multi-input coupling: scaling one robot auto-adjusts the nearest neighbor's scale to conserve total mass. 10) Full undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z) tracking every placement, deletion, scale, rotation, and toggle action. Store state snapshots. Display undo/redo buttons in the UI. Add an HTML overlay panel on the left with: Add Column, Remove, Scale slider, Rotation slider, Sunglasses toggle, mass budget progress bar, undo/redo buttons, and column count. Use a ground plane with grid texture. All robots face the pool center by default.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  robotCount: number,         // total number of robot columns placed in the scene
  selectedRobotIndex: number,         // index of currently selected robot (-1 if none)
  totalMassUsed: number,         // sum of all robot masses (scale^3 per robot)
  massCapacity: number,         // maximum allowed total mass budget (fixed at 50)
  circularPathRadius: number,         // radius of the parametric circle around pool center
  selectedRobotScale: number,         // scale factor of currently selected robot (0.5 to 2.0)
  selectedRobotAngle: number,         // angular position in radians of selected robot on circular path
  poolCenterX: number,         // x-coordinate of pool center position
  poolCenterZ: number,         // z-coordinate of pool center position
  isDragging: boolean,         // whether user is currently dragging a robot along the path
  shadowsEnabled: boolean,         // whether shadow rendering is active
  lastInteractionTimestamp: number,         // milliseconds since last user interaction
}
